title: Daily Social Media Engagement
summary: 15-20 minute daily routine: Meta inbox, comments, growth levers, story sharing

steps.title: Open Meta Business Suite → Inbox
steps.detail: Navigate to the Meta Business Suite inbox to begin the daily engagement session.

steps.title: Respond to new DMs
steps.detail: Reply to all new direct messages. Mark Done when resolved. Leave unread or label if follow-up is required.

steps.title: Review new comments
steps.detail: Like genuine comments. Reply when appropriate. Hide (do not delete) coordinated negative, spam, or extremist comments. Block users with hateful or bot-pattern activity.

steps.title: Growth Lever Execution
steps.detail: Love visible shared posts. Invite reactors to follow page (≤50 reactions: invite all; >50: invite first 50). Prioritize Heart and Cry reactions over Like when capacity is limited.

steps.title: Share to Stories
steps.detail: Share the latest post (or a relevant recent post) to both Facebook Story and Instagram Story. One share per platform per session.

steps.title: Post completion to Slack
steps.detail: Post to #social-media-ops: "SOP-001 complete — DMs: [clear/responded], Comments: [reviewed], Invites: [sent], Stories: [shared]. [date]"

title: Fallen Hero Anniversary Publishing Protocol
summary: End-to-end anniversary memorial post creation and publishing workflow

steps.title: Pull hero data from registry
steps.detail: Retrieve the hero's full name, rank, branch, memorial date, and bio page URL from the Memorial Bracelet Registry.

steps.title: Select or create memorial image
steps.detail: Use existing hero photo or Canva template. Ensure image includes name, rank, branch, and memorial date.

steps.title: Draft memorial post copy
steps.detail: Write the memorial post using the approved template. Include hero info, anniversary year count, charity name, and bio page link.

steps.title: Add hashtags
steps.detail: Include core hashtags (#SteelHearts, #NeverForget, #GoldStarFamily, #HonorTheFallen) plus branch-specific tags.

steps.title: Review and approve
steps.detail: Joseph reviews the complete post (image + copy + hashtags) for accuracy and tone.

steps.title: Publish or schedule
steps.detail: Publish immediately if on the anniversary date, or schedule via Meta Business Suite for the correct date at optimal time.

title: Monthly Memorial Content Planning & Scheduling
summary: Plan and schedule memorial content for upcoming month

steps.title: Pull next month's anniversary list
steps.detail: Query the Memorial Bracelet Registry for all heroes with memorial dates in the upcoming month. Sort by date.

steps.title: Build content calendar
steps.detail: Map each hero to their memorial date. Identify clusters (multiple heroes same week) and plan spacing.

steps.title: Prepare post templates
steps.detail: Generate draft posts for each hero using the Content Generator. Include copy, hashtags, and image notes.

steps.title: Review and assign
steps.detail: Joseph reviews the calendar and assigns any posts to volunteers if applicable.

steps.title: Schedule posts in Meta Business Suite
steps.detail: Schedule approved posts for their target dates. Confirm all are queued correctly.

title: Memorial Commission Intake
summary: Process new bracelet commission requests from inquiry through eligibility verification

steps.title: Initial Response & Hub Task Creation
steps.detail: Acknowledge receipt within 24 hours with empathy. Confirm key details (hero name, branch, date of passing, relationship, memorial type). Check Accounts & Contacts for existing records. Create Commission Pipeline record and hub task in Steel Hearts Tasks.

steps.title: Eligibility Screening
steps.detail: Cross-check against GOV-001 eligibility criteria. Confirm eligibility category (KIA, line of duty, etc.). If unclear, escalate to Joseph. Update status to Eligibility Confirmed or Escalated.

steps.title: Salesforce Record Creation
steps.detail: Search for existing Contact. Create or update Contact and Opportunity (Stage: Intake). Attach all intake correspondence to the Opportunity.

steps.title: Scope & Pricing Confirmation
steps.detail: Define commission scope (type, customization, quantity, special requests). Confirm pricing tier. Send scope and pricing summary to requester.

steps.title: Deposit & Formal Acceptance
steps.detail: Send payment instructions. Upon deposit: update SF Opportunity to Accepted, send confirmation email with timeline estimate, store proof of payment.

steps.title: Handoff to Production
steps.detail: Confirm all assets received (photos, correct spelling, design references). Create Order Queue record. Move SF Opportunity to Production-Active. Update hub task. SOP-INV-001 takes over.

title: Monthly Social Media Performance Review
summary: Export KPIs, analyze trends, compare to benchmarks, report to team

steps.title: Export Meta Business Suite KPIs
steps.detail: Pull 30-day metrics: reach, engagement, follower growth, post performance, story views.

steps.title: Analyze trends
steps.detail: Compare current month to previous months. Identify top-performing posts and content types.

steps.title: Compare to benchmarks
steps.detail: Check metrics against established KPI targets. Flag any metrics below threshold.

steps.title: Generate performance report
steps.detail: Create summary report with key metrics, trends, top posts, and recommendations.

steps.title: Report to team
steps.detail: Share report in #social-media-ops Slack channel. Include in board meeting materials if applicable.

title: Anniversary Remembrance Execution
summary: Full anniversary remembrance cycle: assignment, drafting, review, send

steps.title: Automated Registry Refresh (1st of month)
steps.detail: anniversary-registry-refresh task runs at 9 AM ET. Refreshes Memorial Bracelet Registry from Salesforce. Review count and validation notes.

steps.title: Automated Cycle Generation (2nd of month)
steps.detail: anniversary-cycle-generation creates the monthly work surface. Verify parent task exists in Steel Hearts Tasks. Review generated cycle.

steps.title: Data Verification (Days 1-3)
steps.detail: Chris reviews hero names, ranks, memorial dates, family contact emails. Flag missing data. Confirm record count matches SF report. Update tracker view filter to current month.

steps.title: Volunteer Assignment (Days 3-5)
steps.detail: Chris assigns volunteers in the SHOS app Anniversaries page. Balance workload. Post assignment summary in Slack #anniversary-cycle. Tag volunteers.

steps.title: Email Execution (Throughout Month)
steps.detail: Volunteers execute emails through the Operator. Operator drafts in Gmail using Template A (on-date) or B (catch-up). Volunteer reviews and sends. Tracker updated in real time.

steps.title: Day 15 Checkpoint
steps.detail: 90% target. If behind, post nudge in Slack and email stragglers. Handle any escalations within 48 hours.

steps.title: Board Meeting Prep (Week Before)
steps.detail: Generate two slides: (1) Current Month Recap — completion rate, families reached, escalations. (2) Upcoming Month Preview — assignments, volunteer availability, special cases.

steps.title: Month-End Close
steps.detail: All emails should be complete. 1st-of-month task handles sync-back. Mark parent task in Steel Hearts Tasks as Complete with final completion rate.

title: Anniversary Remembrance — Volunteer Execution Guide
summary: Step-by-step guide for volunteers executing anniversary email sends

steps.title: Connect to the Operator
steps.detail: Open Claude and connect to the SHOS project. Tell the Operator you're ready to send anniversary emails.

steps.title: Review your assignments
steps.detail: Operator fetches your personal filtered view from the Anniversary Tracker. Confirm the count matches expectations.

steps.title: Verify Gmail account
steps.detail: Confirm you're logged into your @steel-hearts.org account (not personal Gmail). Check the profile icon in top-right.

steps.title: Draft email for each hero
steps.detail: Operator drafts using Template A (on-date) or Template B (catch-up) with all placeholders replaced. Review names, rank, and email address.

steps.title: Review and personalize
steps.detail: Check all details are correct. Add any personal touch. You have final say — Operator never sends without approval.

steps.title: Send or schedule
steps.detail: If on-date and >24hrs away: Schedule Send for 9 AM ET on anniversary. If within 24hrs or catch-up: send immediately.

steps.title: Check for additional contacts
steps.detail: If the hero has an additional family contact, the Operator will ask if you want to send to them too. Repeat if yes.

steps.title: Move to next assignment
steps.detail: Repeat for all assignments. Operator posts completion summary to Slack #anniversary-cycle when done.

title: Donated Bracelet Intake
summary: Process donated bracelet requests, verify eligibility, enter into pipeline

steps.title: Intake & Acknowledgment
steps.detail: Email router classifies as DONATED BRACELET REQUEST. Check Accounts & Contacts for existing recipient. Create Contact if needed. Draft compassionate acknowledgment. Create Order Queue record (DON-YYYY-NNN) and hub task in Steel Hearts Tasks.

steps.title: Joseph reviews and sends acknowledgment
steps.detail: Review the drafted response during email triage. Approve and send.

steps.title: Size Confirmation (if needed)
steps.detail: If sizes not specified, draft follow-up email asking for 6-inch vs 7-inch split. Joseph reviews and sends. Update Order Queue when recipient responds.

steps.title: Production Handoff
steps.detail: DON-SF-SYNC zap creates SF Order automatically. Order Queue record progresses through SOP-INV-001: triage → production → Ready to Ship → 3-zap shipping loop.

title: Monthly Financial Operations
summary: Bookkeeper classification review, donation execution, QB reconciliation

steps.title: Export Squarespace Orders (Part A)
steps.detail: Log into Squarespace Commerce → Orders. Set date filter to full prior month. Export CSV. Save as orders_YYYY-MM.csv. (Automated via bookkeeper-classification-report task on 3rd.)

steps.title: Run Classification
steps.detail: Load exported CSV and SKU Master. Apply CSV handling rules (forward-fill, Eastern timezone, count units not rows, exclude tests). Classify each bracelet's $10 obligation as Restricted, Unrestricted, or Pending. Identify D-variant orders.

steps.title: Generate Excel Report
steps.detail: Create workbook with Bookkeeper Summary and Bookkeeper Detail sheets. Color code restricted (amber) and unrestricted (blue). Save as SH_Monthly_Financial_Report_[Month]_[Year].xlsx.

steps.title: Review & Send to Sara
steps.detail: Joseph reviews report — verify total units match Squarespace dashboard. Cross-check restricted totals. Draft email to sara.curran@outlook.com. Joseph reviews and sends.

steps.title: Identify Heroes Due for Donation (Part B)
steps.detail: Filter heroes whose Memorial Date month = prior month. This is the annual cycle completion list. VERIFICATION GATE: hero count must match SF memorial date filter.

steps.title: Calculate Annual Donations
steps.detail: For each hero, count all bracelet sales from 12-month cycle. Multiply units × $10. Group by designated charity. Classify as External, Direct to Family, Internal, Zero Sales, or Pending.

steps.title: Execute Donations
steps.detail: For each external charity: navigate to donation page, make donation, note "In honor of [Hero Names] — Steel Hearts." Tell Operator when each is done. Joseph executes payments manually.

steps.title: Capture Receipts
steps.detail: Operator searches Gmail for confirmation emails. Logs each to SHOS Closeout Queue. Architect batch-attaches to SF Account records and updates Memorial_Bracelet and Account donation fields.

title: Monthly Financial Close
summary: Close monthly books by 5th, generate reports, archive statements

steps.title: Receive classification report from Joseph
steps.detail: Sara receives the monthly Squarespace Order Classifications report email from Joseph (Part A of SOP-FIN-001).

steps.title: Post QuickBooks journal entries
steps.detail: Post restricted obligations to Restricted account. Post unrestricted obligations + D-variant donations to Unrestricted account. One aggregate entry per month each.

steps.title: Bank reconciliation
steps.detail: Reconcile bank accounts in QuickBooks. Ensure all transactions for the month are accounted for.

steps.title: Generate monthly reports
steps.detail: Generate P&L, Balance Sheet, and any other required reports. Archive in QuickBooks and shared drive.

steps.title: Confirm close to Joseph
steps.detail: Email Joseph confirming the monthly books are closed. Note any issues or discrepancies found.

title: Order Fulfillment Production
summary: Bracelet production: laser engraving, packaging, shipping, tracking updates

steps.title: Triage New Orders
steps.detail: Open Order Queue → Active Work Items view. For each order: identify hero and size variant, check pre-made inventory in Salesforce.

steps.title: Route by Fulfillment Method
steps.detail: Pre-made in stock → Pre-Made Pull, set Ready to Ship. No stock but design exists → Laser Production, set Ready to Laser. No stock and no design → Design + Laser, set Design Needed.

steps.title: Create Graphic Design Request (if needed)
steps.detail: Create entry in Graphic Design Tracker with hero details. Assign to designer. Wait for completed file to be attached to SF Memorial_Bracelet record.

steps.title: Laser Production
steps.detail: Pull blank bracelet in correct size. Open design file from Google Drive (via SHOS app Laser page). Laser engrave. Update production status to Ready to Ship in SHOS app.

steps.title: Ready to Ship → Automation
steps.detail: Setting Ready to Ship in SHOS app auto-pushes to ShipStation (for donated orders). Kristin prints labels, packages, ships. Shipping notification email auto-sends to customer. Slack notifies ops hub.

steps.title: Verify completion
steps.detail: Confirm order status shows Shipped in SHOS app. Verify ShipStation tracking number populated. Mark weekly hub task complete when all orders in batch are done.

title: Automated Notion-SF Reconciliation (DEPRECATED)
summary: DEPRECATED — Notion and Zapier retired 2026-03-28. Supabase is primary DB with nightly SF sync via /api/sync cron.

steps.title: Review sync status
steps.detail: Check that Notion↔SF sync zaps ran successfully this week. Review any error notifications.

steps.title: Compare record counts
steps.detail: Compare key record counts between Notion and Salesforce (heroes, orders, contacts). Flag discrepancies.

steps.title: Resolve discrepancies
steps.detail: For any mismatches, determine which system is authoritative and correct the other. Log resolutions.

title: Monthly Supporter Message Delivery Protocol
summary: Package, review, and deliver supporter messages to Gold Star families

steps.title: Pull undelivered messages from Salesforce
steps.detail: Query Family_Message__c for messages with Status = New or Ready for Delivery. Group by hero/family.

steps.title: Apply text normalization
steps.detail: Clean message text per FM-STD-004 standards. Fix formatting, remove duplicates, standardize punctuation.

steps.title: Build message packets
steps.detail: Package messages per FM-STD-003 format. One packet per family, grouped by hero. Include sender info and message content.

steps.title: Review packets for quality
steps.detail: Joseph reviews each packet for accuracy, tone, and completeness. Flag any messages that need attention.

steps.title: Deliver to families
steps.detail: Send message packets to family contacts via email. Use compassionate, personal tone. Update Status to Delivered in Salesforce.

steps.title: Log delivery
steps.detail: Update all Family_Message__c records to Delivered with delivery date. Note any bounces or issues.

title: Supporter Message Packet Standard
summary: Standard format and quality requirements for family message packets

title: Supporter Message Text Normalization Standard
summary: Text cleanup rules for supporter messages before delivery

title: Supporter Message Routing Schema
summary: Routing logic for how supporter messages flow from intake to family delivery

title: SOP Governance & Registry Enforcement Framework
summary: Rules for SOP versioning, promotion, deprecation, and registry enforcement

title: Message Delivery as Mission Doctrine
summary: Founding doctrine: automation supports compassion, never replaces it

title: Donor Stewardship Execution
summary: Weekly donor thank-you workflow: review new donations, compose personalized emails, track stewardship status

steps.title: Open SHOS Donor Engagement Page
steps.detail: Navigate to SHOS → Donor Engagement. The page loads all donations from Salesforce Donation__c in real time.

steps.title: Filter to Donors Needing Thanks
steps.detail: Click 'Needs Thanks' in the Status filter. Optionally narrow by 'Last 30 Days' to focus on recent donations. Note the count — this is your working list.

steps.title: Review Each Donor
steps.detail: For each donor: note their name, email, amount, date, and whether they're a repeat donor. If no email is on file, flag for Joseph to investigate the Salesforce Contact record.

steps.title: Compose Thank-You Email
steps.detail: Click 'compose email' on the donor's row to open a pre-filled template in your mail client. Personalize: acknowledge repeat donors, reference large gift impact ($100+ ≈ 10 bracelets), welcome first-time donors. Send from @steel-hearts.org account.

steps.title: Send and Mark as Thanked
steps.detail: Send the email. Then click 'Send Thanks' in SHOS — button changes to 'Thanked' (green). Repeat for all donors in the queue.

steps.title: Handle Escalations
steps.detail: Large donations ($500+): Joseph sends personally. No email on file: flag for Joseph. Bounced email: update SF record. Donor questions: forward to joseph@steel-hearts.org.

steps.title: Post Completion to Slack
steps.detail: Post to #ops-hub: 'SOP-DON-002 complete — [X] donor thank-yous sent. [date]'
